content.full_text:
BEFORE THE FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION


In the Matter of:                          )
                                           )  No. 75-047
Opinion requested by                       )  August 7, 1975
State Controller Kenneth Cory              )
                                           )


BY THE COMMISSION: We have been asked the following question
by Kenneth Cory, Controller of the State of California:

     Mr. Cory has received an unsolicited parking pass from the
office of governmental affairs of the California State Universities
and Colleges for use while on official business at any California
State University or College campus. Mr. Cory does not know the
monetary value of this pass.

     Under the requirements established by Government Code
Section 82028,1/ is this a reportable gift?

                              CONCLUSION

     The parking pass is a reportable gift under the requirements of the Political Reform Act.

                              ANALYSIS

     The Political Reform Act provides that a gift is:

     ...any payment to the extent that consideration of equal or greater value is not received.
Section 82028.

     Payment includes the

     ...rendering of money, property, services or
anything else of value, whether tangible or
intangible. Section 82044.

     The pass here permits its holder to park in the student lots,
the staff and administration lots and in some cases the visitor

_______________________
1/ All statutory references are to the Government Code unless
   otherwise noted.

lots of any of the nineteen state universities and colleges. The
cost for a year's pass to the staff and administration lots of
one of the campuses averages approximately thirty dollars, according to university personnel.

The parking pass given to Mr. Cory is a gift if it is a thing
of value for which consideration was not received. The pass con-
fers a benefit in that any person parking on those campuses is
otherwise required to pay for parking privileges. The basis for
calculation of value is the fair market value of the item. Sec-
tion 81011. Fair market value requires a willing buyer and a
willing seller. Kaiser Co. v. Reid, 30 C.2d 610 (1947).

Since the separate campuses are able to sell passes for
single campuses for an average of thirty dollars, the value of a
pass which is valid at all the campuses is certainly worth more
than that amount. The Political Reform Act requires that gifts
of twenty-five dollars or more must be reported; thus, the pass
in question is a reportable gift. Section 8720(a)(1).

Approved by the Commission on August 7, 1975. Concurring:
Brosnahan, Carpenter, Lowenstein, Miller and Waters.

[signature]
Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman

sections.conclusion: The parking pass is a reportable gift under the requirements of the Political Reform Act.

Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman
